content: The adversarial auditor-per-phase pattern (one auditor specialist embedded in each coordinator) is highly effective for creative domains with many invariants. With 81 invariants distributed across 8 phase auditors, each auditor enforces only the invariants relevant to its phase's output. The distribution was well-shaped: most invariants had 3-5 agent references, with only 8 single-reference invariants (each correctly scoped). The universal invariant (INV-030) was referenced by all 67 agents. This distribution pattern ensures no single agent is overburdened while no invariant falls through the cracks.
sourceEvidence: Category 2 (Invariant Enforcement): 81 invariants checked, 0 gaps. Distribution: 8 invariants with 1 ref, 8 with 2, 11 with 3, 13 with 4, 16 with 5, 10 with 6, 4 with 7, 4 with 8... All 8 single-reference invariants confirmed correctly scoped. Clean since cycle 1.

history.reason: Cold start — first observation from initial factory run
